title: Matthew 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 This is the family record of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham:
2 Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
3 Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar. Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Ram.
4 Ram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
5 Salmon was the father of Boaz by Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed by Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse.
6 Jesse was the father of David the king. Next: David was the father of Solomon by the wife of Uriah.
7 Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asa.
8 Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram. Joram was the father of Uzziah.
9 Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
10 Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amon. Amon was the father of Josiah.
11 Josiah was the father of Jeconiah and his brothers, at the time of the exile to Babylon.
12 After the exile to Babylon: Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
13 Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
14 Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Achim. Achim was the father of Eliud.
15 Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
16 Jacob was the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary. And from Mary was born Jesus, who is called Christ.
17 So in all, there were fourteen generations from Abraham to David. There were fourteen from David to the exile to Babylon. And there were fourteen from the exile to the Christ.

18 This is how the birth of Jesus Christ happened. His mother Mary was promised in marriage to Joseph. But before they came together as husband and wife, she was found to be pregnant through the Holy Spirit.
19 Joseph her husband was a righteous man. He did not want to shame her in public. So he decided to divorce her quietly.

20 But after he had thought about these things, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, "Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife. The child in her is from the Holy Spirit.
21 She will give birth to a Son, and you must give him the name Jesus. He will save his people from their sins."
22 All this happened so that what the Lord had said through the prophet would come true:
23 "Look, the virgin will be pregnant and will give birth to a son. They will call him Immanuel" — which means, "God with us."
24 When Joseph woke up, he did what the angel of the Lord had told him. He took Mary as his wife.
25 But he did not sleep with her until she gave birth to a Son. And Joseph gave him the name Jesus.

title: Matthew 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod. After this, Magi (wise men from the east) arrived in Jerusalem.
2 They asked, "Where is the one who has been born King of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him."
3 When King Herod heard this, he was troubled. All Jerusalem was troubled with him.
4 He called together all the chief priests and scribes of the people. He asked them where the Christ was to be born.
5 "In Bethlehem in Judea," they replied. "For this is what the prophet has written:
6 'But you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are not at all the smallest among the rulers of Judah. For out of you will come a ruler who will be the shepherd of my people Israel.'"
7 Then Herod called the Magi in secret. He learned from them the exact time when the star had appeared.
8 He sent them to Bethlehem. He said, "Go and search carefully for the child. When you find him, tell me. Then I too may go and worship him."
9 After they had listened to the king, they went on their way. The star they had seen in the east went ahead of them. It stopped over the place where the child was.
10 When they saw the star, they were very, very happy.
11 They came to the house and saw the child with his mother Mary. They fell down and worshiped him. Then they opened their treasures and gave him gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh.
12 They were warned in a dream not to go back to Herod. So they went home to their country by another road.

13 When the Magi had gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream. "Get up!" the angel said. "Take the child and his mother and flee to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you. Herod is going to search for the child to kill him."
14 So Joseph got up. He took the child and his mother during the night and went to Egypt.
15 He stayed there until the death of Herod. This made true what the Lord had said through the prophet: "Out of Egypt I called my Son."

16 When Herod saw that the Magi had tricked him, he was full of anger. He sent orders to kill all the boys in Bethlehem and around it who were two years old and under. He did this based on the time he had learned from the Magi.
17 Then what was spoken through the prophet Jeremiah came true:
18 "A voice is heard in Ramah — weeping and great mourning. Rachel is crying for her children. She would not be comforted, because they were gone."
19 After Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.
20 "Get up!" the angel said. "Take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel. Those who were trying to kill the child are now dead."
21 So Joseph got up. He took the child and his mother and went to the land of Israel.
22 But Joseph heard that Archelaus was now ruling in Judea in place of his father Herod. He was afraid to go there. He was warned in a dream, so he went away to the area of Galilee.
23 He went and lived in a town called Nazareth. So was made true what was spoken through the prophets: "He will be called a Nazarene."

title: Matthew 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 In those days, John the Baptist came. He was preaching in the desert of Judea.
2 He said, "Turn from your sins, for the kingdom of heaven is near."
3 John was the one spoken about through the prophet Isaiah: "A voice will call out in the desert: 'Prepare the way for the Lord. Make straight paths for him.'"
4 John wore clothes made of camel hair, with a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.
5 People went out to him from Jerusalem and all Judea and the whole area around the Jordan.
6 They confessed their sins and were baptized by him in the Jordan River.
7 But John saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to where he was baptizing. He said to them, "You family of snakes! Who warned you to escape from the coming anger of God?
8 So show by what you do that you have turned from sin.
9 Do not think to yourselves, 'We have Abraham as our father.' I tell you, God can raise up children for Abraham out of these stones.
10 The axe is ready at the root of the trees. Every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.
11 I baptize you with water for repentance. But after me will come one more powerful than I am. I am not worthy even to carry his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
12 His winnowing fork is in his hand to clear his threshing floor. He will gather his wheat into the barn, but he will burn up the chaff with fire that never goes out."
13 Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John.
14 But John tried to stop him. He said, "I need to be baptized by you, and yet you come to me?"
15 "Let it be so for now," Jesus replied. "It is right for us to do everything that is right this way." Then John let him.
16 As soon as Jesus was baptized, he went up out of the water. Suddenly, heaven was opened. He saw the Spirit of God coming down like a dove and resting on him.
17 And a voice from heaven said, "This is my beloved Son, in whom I am very pleased!"

title: Matthew 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the desert. There he was tested by the devil.
2 After Jesus went without food for forty days and forty nights, he was hungry.
3 The tempter came to him and said, "If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread."
4 But Jesus answered, "It is written: 'People do not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.'"
5 Then the devil took him to the holy city and set him on the highest point of the temple.

6 "If you are the Son of God," he said, "throw yourself down. For it is written: 'He will tell his angels about you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not hit your foot on a stone.'"
7 Jesus replied, "It is also written: 'Do not test the Lord your God.'"
8 Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain. He showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
9 "All this I will give you," he said, "if you will fall down and worship me."
10 "Get away from me, Satan!" Jesus declared. "For it is written: 'Worship the Lord your God and serve him only.'"
11 Then the devil left him, and angels came and took care of him.
12 When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went back to Galilee.
13 Leaving Nazareth, he went and lived in Capernaum. Capernaum is by the sea in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.
14 This was to make true what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:
15 "Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, the Way of the Sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles —
16 the people living in darkness have seen a great light. On those living in the land of the shadow of death, a light has come."
17 From that time on, Jesus began to preach, "Turn from your sins, for the kingdom of heaven is near."
18 Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee. He saw two brothers — Simon (called Peter) and his brother Andrew. They were throwing a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.
19 "Come, follow me," Jesus said, "and I will make you fishers of people."
20 At once they left their nets and followed him.
21 Going on from there, Jesus saw two other brothers — James, son of Zebedee, and his brother John. They were in a boat with their father Zebedee, fixing their nets. Jesus called them.
22 Right away they left the boat and their father, and followed him.
23 Jesus went throughout Galilee. He taught in their synagogues, preached the gospel of the kingdom, and healed every disease and sickness among the people.

24 News about him spread all over Syria. People brought to him all who were sick with many different diseases. They brought those with strong pain, people who had demons in them, those with seizures, and the paralyzed. Jesus healed them all.
25 Large crowds followed him. They came from Galilee, the Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and the area beyond the Jordan.

title: Matthew 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the mountain and sat down. His disciples came to him.
2 He began to teach them, saying:
3 "Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
4 Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.
5 Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.
6 Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for what is right, for they will be filled.
7 Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy.
8 Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.
9 Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called sons of God.
10 Blessed are those who are persecuted because they do what is right, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
11 Blessed are you when people insult you, persecute you, and lie about you saying all kinds of evil things — because of me.
12 Be glad and full of joy, because great is your reward in heaven. For in the same way they persecuted the prophets who lived before you.
13 You are the salt of the earth. But if salt loses its salty taste, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything — only to be thrown out and stepped on by people.
14 You are the light of the world. A city on a hill cannot be hidden.
15 People do not light a lamp and put it under a basket. Instead, they put it on a stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house.
16 In the same way, let your light shine before people. Then they may see your good deeds and give glory to your Father in heaven.
17 Do not think that I have come to do away with the Law or the Prophets. I have not come to do away with them, but to make them complete.
18 I tell you the truth: until heaven and earth pass away, not the smallest letter or mark will disappear from the Law, until everything is done.

19 So whoever breaks even the smallest of these commandments — and teaches others to do the same — will be called least in the kingdom of heaven. But whoever practices and teaches them will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
20 I tell you: unless your right living is greater than that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
21 You have heard that it was said to the people long ago, 'Do not murder,' and, 'Anyone who murders will be judged.'

22 But I tell you: anyone who is angry with his brother will be judged. Anyone who says to his brother, 'Raca' (an insult), will be brought before the Sanhedrin. But anyone who says, 'You fool!' will be in danger of the fire of hell.
23 So if you are offering your gift at the altar, and there you remember that your brother has something against you,
24 leave your gift there before the altar. First go and make peace with your brother. Then come and offer your gift.

25 Make peace quickly with your enemy while you are still on the way to court. Otherwise, he may hand you over to the judge. The judge may hand you over to the officer, and you may be thrown into prison.
26 I tell you the truth: you will not get out until you have paid the last coin.
27 You have heard that it was said, 'Do not commit adultery.'
28 But I tell you: anyone who looks at a woman with lust has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
29 If your right eye makes you sin, tear it out and throw it away. It is better to lose one part of your body than to have your whole body thrown into hell.
30 And if your right hand makes you sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better to lose one part of your body than to have your whole body go into hell.
31 It has also been said, 'Whoever divorces his wife must give her a certificate of divorce.'
32 But I tell you: anyone who divorces his wife, except for sexual sin, causes her to commit adultery. And whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
33 Again, you have heard that it was said to the people long ago, 'Do not break your promise, but keep your promises to the Lord.'
34 But I tell you: do not swear at all — not by heaven, for it is God's throne;
35 not by the earth, for it is his footstool; not by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
36 Do not swear by your head, because you cannot make a single hair white or black.
37 Simply let your 'Yes' be 'Yes,' and your 'No,' 'No.' Anything more than this comes from the evil one.
38 You have heard that it was said, 'Eye for eye and tooth for tooth.'
39 But I tell you: do not fight back against an evil person. If someone slaps you on your right cheek, turn the other to him too.
40 If someone wants to sue you and take your shirt, let him have your coat as well.
41 If someone forces you to go one mile, go with him two miles.
42 Give to the one who asks you. Do not turn away from the one who wants to borrow from you.
43 You have heard that it was said, 'Love your neighbor' and 'Hate your enemy.'
44 But I tell you: love your enemies, and pray for those who persecute you.
45 Then you will be sons of your Father in heaven. He makes his sun rise on the evil and the good. He sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.
46 If you love only those who love you, what reward will you get? Don't even the tax collectors do the same?
47 And if you greet only your brothers, what are you doing more than others? Don't even the Gentiles do the same?
48 So be perfect, as your heavenly Father is perfect."

title: Matthew 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 "Be careful not to do your acts of righteousness in front of people just to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven.

2 So when you give to the poor, do not blow a trumpet ahead of you. That is what the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honored by people. I tell you the truth: they already have all their reward.
3 But when you give to the poor, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing.
4 Your giving will be in secret. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.

5 When you pray, do not be like the hypocrites. They love to pray while standing in the synagogues and on the street corners, so they will be seen by people. I tell you the truth: they already have all their reward.
6 But when you pray, go into your private room and shut the door. Pray to your Father, who is unseen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
7 When you pray, do not repeat empty words like pagans. They think they will be heard because of their many words.
8 Do not be like them. Your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
9 So this is how you should pray: 'Our Father in heaven, may your name be honored as holy.
10 May your kingdom come. May your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.
11 Give us today our daily bread.
12 And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven those who owe us.
13 And do not let us be tested, but save us from the evil one.'
14 For if you forgive people their wrongs, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
15 But if you do not forgive people their wrongs, neither will your Father forgive your wrongs.
16 When you fast, do not be gloomy like the hypocrites. They make their faces look bad to show people they are fasting. I tell you the truth: they already have all their reward.
17 But when you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face,
18 so that your fasting will not be obvious to people, but only to your Father, who is unseen. And your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
19 Do not store up treasures for yourselves on earth. On earth, moths and rust destroy them, and thieves break in and steal them.
20 But store up treasures for yourselves in heaven. There, moths and rust do not destroy them, and thieves do not break in and steal them.
21 For where your treasure is, there your heart will also be.
22 The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are good, your whole body will be full of light.
23 But if your eyes are bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great that darkness is!
24 No one can serve two masters. Either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be loyal to the one and hate the other. You cannot serve both God and money.
25 So I tell you: do not worry about your life — what you will eat or drink. Do not worry about your body — what you will wear. Isn't life more than food, and the body more than clothes?
26 Look at the birds in the sky. They do not plant or harvest or gather into barns. Yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Aren't you much more valuable than they are?
27 Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?
28 And why do you worry about clothes? Look at how the flowers of the field grow. They do not work hard or make cloth.
29 Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his beautiful clothes was dressed like one of these.
30 That is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the fire. Will he not much more clothe you, you who have such little faith?
31 So do not worry, saying, 'What shall we eat?' or 'What shall we drink?' or 'What shall we wear?'
32 The Gentiles run after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
33 But seek first the kingdom of God and his right way, and all these things will be added to you.
34 So do not worry about tomorrow. Tomorrow will worry about itself. Today has enough trouble of its own."

title: Matthew 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 "Do not judge, or you will be judged.
2 For with the same judgment you give, you will be judged. And with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
3 Why do you look at the speck in your brother's eye, but fail to see the log in your own eye?
4 How can you say to your brother, 'Let me take the speck out of your eye,' when there is still a log in your own eye?
5 You hypocrite! First take the log out of your own eye. Then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother's eye.
6 Do not give to dogs what is holy. Do not throw your pearls before pigs. If you do, they may step on them, then turn and tear you apart.
7 Ask, and it will be given to you. Seek, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened to you.
8 For everyone who asks, receives. The one who seeks, finds. And to the one who knocks, the door will be opened.
9 Which of you, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
10 Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?
11 So if you, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
12 So in everything, do to others what you want them to do to you. For this is the heart of the Law and the Prophets.
13 Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many enter through it.
14 But small is the gate and narrow is the way that leads to life, and only a few find it.
15 Watch out for false prophets. They come to you in sheep's clothing, but inside they are hungry wolves.
16 By their fruit you will know them. Are grapes gathered from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
17 In the same way, every good tree produces good fruit. But a bad tree produces bad fruit.
18 A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
19 Every tree that does not produce good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
20 So by their fruit you will know them.
21 Not everyone who says to me, 'Lord, Lord,' will enter the kingdom of heaven. Only those who do what my Father in heaven wants will enter.
22 Many will say to me on that day, 'Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name? Did we not drive out demons in your name and do many miracles?'
23 Then I will tell them plainly, 'I never knew you. Get away from me, you people who break the law!'
24 So everyone who hears these words of mine and does what they say is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
25 The rain fell. The floods came. The winds blew and beat against that house. But it did not fall, because its foundation was on the rock.
26 But everyone who hears these words of mine and does not do what they say is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
27 The rain fell. The floods came. The winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell — and it fell with a great crash!"
28 When Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
29 He taught as one who had authority, and not like their scribes.

title: Matthew 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
2 Suddenly, a man with leprosy came and knelt before him. He said, "Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean."
3 Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. "I am willing," he said. "Be clean!" Right away the leprosy was cleansed.
4 Then Jesus told him, "See that you don't tell anyone. But go, show yourself to the priest. Offer the gift Moses commanded, as proof to them."
5 When Jesus entered Capernaum, a centurion came and pleaded with him.
6 "Lord," he said, "my servant lies at home, paralyzed and in terrible pain."
7 "I will go and heal him," Jesus replied.
8 The centurion answered, "Lord, I am not worthy to have you come into my house. But just say the word, and my servant will be healed.
9 I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell one to go, and he goes. I tell another to come, and he comes. I tell my servant to do something, and he does it."
10 When Jesus heard this, he was amazed. He said to those following him, "I tell you the truth: I have not found anyone in Israel with such great faith.
11 I tell you that many will come from the east and the west. They will sit at the table with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
12 But the sons of the kingdom will be thrown into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth."
13 Then Jesus said to the centurion, "Go! As you have believed, so it will be done for you." And his servant was healed at that very hour.
14 When Jesus arrived at Peter's house, he saw Peter's mother-in-law sick in bed with a fever.
15 He touched her hand, and the fever left her. She got up and began to serve him.
16 When evening came, many who had demons in them were brought to Jesus. He drove out the spirits with a word and healed all the sick.
17 This was to make true what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah: "He took our weaknesses and carried our diseases."
18 When Jesus saw a large crowd around him, he gave orders to cross to the other side of the sea.
19 One of the scribes came to him and said, "Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go."
20 Jesus replied, "Foxes have dens, and birds in the sky have nests. But the Son of Man has no place to lay his head."
21 Another of his disciples said, "Lord, first let me go and bury my father."
22 But Jesus told him, "Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead."
23 When Jesus got into the boat, his disciples followed him.
24 Suddenly a violent storm came up on the sea. The waves were covering the boat. But Jesus was sleeping.
25 The disciples went and woke him. "Lord, save us!" they said. "We are going to die!"
26 "You of little faith," Jesus replied, "why are you so afraid?" Then he got up and ordered the winds and the sea, and it was totally calm.
27 The men were amazed and asked, "What kind of man is this? Even the winds and the sea obey him!"
28 When Jesus arrived on the other side in the area of the Gadarenes, he was met by two men with demons in them. They came from the tombs. They were so violent that no one could pass that way.
29 "What do you want with us, Son of God?" they shouted. "Have you come here to hurt us before the set time?"
30 In the distance, a large herd of pigs was feeding.
31 The demons begged Jesus, "If you drive us out, send us into the herd of pigs."
32 "Go!" he told them. So they came out and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and died in the waters.
33 Those who were watching the pigs ran off into the town. They reported all this, including what had happened to the men with demons.
34 Then the whole town went out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their area.

title: Matthew 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 Jesus got into a boat, crossed over, and came to his own town.
2 Just then some men brought a paralyzed man to him, lying on a mat. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, "Be brave, son. Your sins are forgiven."
3 Some of the scribes saw this and said to themselves, "This man is blaspheming!"
4 But Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said, "Why do you hold evil in your hearts?
5 Which is easier — to say, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'?
6 But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right on earth to forgive sins." Then he said to the paralyzed man, "Get up. Pick up your mat and go home."
7 And the man got up and went home.
8 When the crowds saw this, they were filled with awe. They gave glory to God, who had given such authority to men.
9 As Jesus went on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax booth. "Follow me," he told him. Matthew got up and followed him.
10 Later, as Jesus was eating at Matthew's house, many tax collectors and sinners came and ate with him and his disciples.
11 When the Pharisees saw this, they asked his disciples, "Why does your Teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?"
12 Jesus heard this and said, "Healthy people don't need a doctor — sick people do.
13 Go and learn what this means: 'I want mercy, not sacrifice.' For I did not come to call righteous people, but sinners."
14 Then John's disciples came to Jesus. They asked, "Why is it that we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?"
15 Jesus replied, "How can the guests of the bridegroom mourn while he is with them? But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them. Then they will fast.
16 No one sews a patch of new cloth on an old coat. The patch will pull away from the coat, and the tear will be worse.

17 No one pours new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the skins will burst, the wine will spill out, and the wineskins will be ruined. New wine is poured into new wineskins, and both are kept safe."
18 While Jesus was saying these things, a synagogue leader came and knelt before him. "My daughter has just died," he said. "But come and put your hand on her, and she will live."
19 So Jesus got up and went with him, along with his disciples.
20 Suddenly, a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus. She touched the edge of his coat.
21 She said to herself, "If I only touch his coat, I will be healed."
22 Jesus turned and saw her. "Be brave, daughter," he said, "your faith has healed you." And the woman was healed from that very hour.
23 When Jesus came to the house of the synagogue leader, he saw the flute players and the noisy crowd.
24 "Go away," he told them. "The girl is not dead. She is sleeping." And they laughed at him.
25 After the crowd was put outside, Jesus went in and took the girl by the hand. She got up.
26 And the news about this spread throughout that area.
27 As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed him. They cried out, "Have mercy on us, Son of David!"
28 When Jesus had entered the house, the blind men came to him. "Do you believe that I am able to do this?" he asked. "Yes, Lord," they answered.
29 Then he touched their eyes and said, "According to your faith, it will be done for you."
30 And their eyes were opened. Jesus warned them strongly, "See that no one finds out about this!"
31 But they went out and spread the news about him throughout the land.
32 As they were leaving, a man with a demon in him who could not speak was brought to Jesus.
33 When the demon had been driven out, the man began to speak. The crowds were amazed and said, "Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel!"
34 But the Pharisees said, "It is by the prince of demons that he drives out demons."
35 Jesus went through all the towns and villages. He taught in their synagogues, preached the gospel of the kingdom, and healed every disease and sickness.
36 When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were tired and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
37 Then he said to his disciples, "The harvest is plenty, but the workers are few.
38 Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest."

title: Matthew 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 Jesus called his twelve disciples to him. He gave them power over evil spirits, so that they could drive them out. He also gave them power to heal every disease and sickness.
2 These are the names of the twelve apostles: first, Simon (called Peter) and his brother Andrew; James son of Zebedee, and his brother John;
3 Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus;
4 Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus.
5 Jesus sent these twelve out with these instructions: "Do not go onto the road of the Gentiles. Do not enter any town of the Samaritans.
6 Instead, go to the lost sheep of Israel.
7 As you go, preach this message: 'The kingdom of heaven is near.'
8 Heal the sick. Raise the dead. Heal those with leprosy. Drive out demons. Freely you have received; freely give.
9 Do not carry any gold or silver or copper coins in your belts.
10 Take no bag for the road, or a second shirt, or sandals, or a walking stick. For the worker deserves his food.
11 Whichever town or village you enter, find out who is worthy. Stay at his house until you leave.
12 When you enter the home, greet the people in it.
13 If the home is worthy, let your peace rest on it. But if it is not worthy, let your peace return to you.
14 If anyone will not welcome you or listen to your words, shake the dust off your feet when you leave that home or town.
15 I tell you the truth: it will be easier for Sodom and Gomorrah on the day of judgment than for that town.
16 Look, I am sending you out like sheep among wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as gentle as doves.
17 Watch out for people. They will hand you over to their councils and whip you in their synagogues.
18 Because of me, you will be brought before governors and kings. You will be witnesses to them and to the Gentiles.
19 But when they hand you over, do not worry about how to respond or what to say. At that time, you will be given what to say.
20 It will not be you speaking, but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.
21 Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child. Children will rise against their parents and have them put to death.
22 Everyone will hate you because of my name. But the one who keeps going to the end will be saved.
23 When they persecute you in one town, flee to the next. I tell you the truth: you will not reach all the towns of Israel before the Son of Man comes.
24 A disciple is not above his teacher, and a servant is not above his master.

25 It is enough for a disciple to be like his teacher, and a servant like his master. If the head of the house has been called Beelzebul, how much more will the members of his household be called names!
26 So do not be afraid of them. There is nothing hidden that will not be made known.
27 What I tell you in the dark, speak in the daylight. What is whispered in your ear, proclaim from the rooftops.
28 Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Instead, fear the One who can destroy both soul and body in hell.
29 Aren't two sparrows sold for a penny? Yet not one of them will fall to the ground apart from the will of your Father.
30 And even the very hairs of your head are all counted.
31 So do not be afraid. You are worth more than many sparrows.
32 So everyone who confesses me before people, I will also confess him before my Father in heaven.
33 But whoever denies me before people, I will also deny him before my Father in heaven.
34 Do not think that I have come to bring peace to the earth. I have not come to bring peace, but a sword.
35 I have come to turn 'a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.
36 A man's enemies will be the members of his own household.'
37 Anyone who loves his father or mother more than me is not worthy of me. Anyone who loves his son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
38 Anyone who does not take up his cross and follow me is not worthy of me.
39 Whoever finds his life will lose it. Whoever loses his life for my sake will find it.
40 Whoever receives you receives me. Whoever receives me receives the One who sent me.
41 Whoever receives a prophet because he is a prophet will get a prophet's reward. Whoever receives a righteous man because he is a righteous man will get a righteous man's reward.
42 If anyone gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones because he is my disciple — I tell you the truth, he will never lose his reward."

title: Matthew 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
2 Meanwhile John, who was in prison, heard about the works of Christ. He sent his disciples
3 to ask Jesus, "Are you the One who was to come, or should we look for someone else?"
4 Jesus replied, "Go back and report to John what you hear and see:
5 The blind receive their sight. The lame walk. Those with leprosy are healed. The deaf hear. The dead are raised. And the good news is preached to the poor.
6 Blessed is the one who does not fall away because of me."
7 As John's disciples were leaving, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: "What did you go out into the desert to see? A reed shaking in the wind?
8 What did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? Look, those who wear fine clothing are found in kings' palaces.
9 Then what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you — and more than a prophet.
10 This is the one about whom it is written: 'I will send my messenger ahead of you. He will prepare your way before you.'
11 I tell you the truth: among those born of women, no one greater than John the Baptist has ever come. Yet even the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he is.
12 From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been facing violence. And the violent are forcing their way into it.
13 For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.
14 And if you are willing to accept it, he is the Elijah who was to come.
15 Whoever has ears, let him hear.
16 To what can I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling out to others:
17 'We played the flute for you, but you did not dance. We sang a sad song, but you did not mourn.'
18 John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, 'He has a demon!'

19 The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, 'Look at this man — he eats too much and drinks too much! He is a friend of tax collectors and sinners!' But wisdom is proven right by her actions."
20 Then Jesus began to speak against the cities where most of his miracles had been done, because they did not turn from sin.
21 "How terrible for you, Chorazin! How terrible for you, Bethsaida! If the miracles done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have turned from sin long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
22 But I tell you, it will be easier for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.
23 And you, Capernaum — will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades! If the miracles done in you had been done in Sodom, it would still be here today.
24 But I tell you that it will be easier for Sodom on the day of judgment than for you."
25 At that time Jesus said, "I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth. You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and you have shown them to little children.
26 Yes, Father — this was pleasing in your sight.
27 All things have been given to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father. And no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
28 Come to me, all you who are tired and carrying heavy loads, and I will give you rest.
29 Take my yoke upon you and learn from me. For I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
30 For my yoke is easy and my burden is light."

title: Matthew 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 At that time, Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry. They began to pick the heads of grain and eat them.
2 When the Pharisees saw this, they said to him, "Look, your disciples are doing what is not allowed on the Sabbath."
3 Jesus replied, "Have you not read what David did when he and his men were hungry?
4 He entered the house of God. He and his men ate the holy bread, which was not lawful for them to eat — only for the priests.
5 Or haven't you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple break the Sabbath, and yet they are not guilty?
6 But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
7 If only you had known the meaning of 'I want mercy, not sacrifice,' you would not have condemned the innocent.
8 For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath."
9 Moving on from there, Jesus entered their synagogue.
10 A man with a crippled hand was there. To accuse Jesus, they asked him, "Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?"
11 He replied, "If one of you has a sheep and it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, won't you take hold of it and lift it out?
12 How much more valuable is a man than a sheep! So it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath."
13 Then Jesus said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." He stretched it out, and it was fully healed, just like the other.
14 But the Pharisees went out and planned how they might kill Jesus.
15 Jesus knew about this and went away from there. Large crowds followed him, and he healed them all.
16 He warned them not to make him known.
17 This was to make true what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:
18 "Here is my Servant, whom I have chosen, my beloved, in whom my soul delights. I will put my Spirit on him, and he will bring justice to the nations.
19 He will not argue or cry out. No one will hear his voice in the streets.
20 A bruised reed he will not break, and a smoking wick he will not put out, until he brings justice to victory.
21 In his name the nations will put their hope."
22 Then a man with a demon, who was blind and could not speak, was brought to Jesus. Jesus healed the man, so that he could speak and see.
23 The crowds were amazed and asked, "Could this be the Son of David?"
24 But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, "Only by Beelzebul, the prince of demons, does this man drive out demons."
25 Jesus knew their thoughts. He said to them, "Every kingdom divided against itself will be destroyed. Every city or household divided against itself will not stand.
26 If Satan drives out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then can his kingdom stand?
27 And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons drive them out? So they will be your judges.
28 But if I drive out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come on you.
29 How can anyone enter a strong man's house and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take from his house.
30 Whoever is not with me is against me. Whoever does not gather with me scatters.
31 So I tell you: every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men. But blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
32 Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven. But whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven — neither in this age nor in the one to come.
33 Make a tree good and its fruit will be good. Or make a tree bad and its fruit will be bad. For a tree is known by its fruit.
34 You family of snakes, how can you who are evil say anything good? For out of the overflow of the heart, the mouth speaks.
35 A good person brings good things out of the good treasure inside. An evil person brings evil things out of the evil treasure inside.
36 I tell you: people will answer on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken.
37 By your words you will be found not guilty, and by your words you will be condemned."
38 Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to him, "Teacher, we want to see a sign from you."
39 Jesus replied, "An evil and unfaithful generation demands a sign. But none will be given it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
40 For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so the Son of Man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
41 The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it. They turned from sin at the preaching of Jonah. And now One greater than Jonah is here.
42 The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it. She came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon. And now One greater than Solomon is here.
43 When an evil spirit comes out of a person, it passes through dry places looking for rest, but does not find it.
44 Then it says, 'I will return to the house I left.' When it returns, it finds the house empty, swept clean, and put in order.

45 Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more evil than itself. They go in and live there. And the final state of that person is worse than the first. So it will be with this evil generation."
46 While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers stood outside. They wanted to speak to him.
47 Someone told him, "Look, your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to speak to you."
48 But Jesus replied, "Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?"
49 Pointing to his disciples, he said, "Here are my mother and my brothers.
50 For whoever does what my Father in heaven wants is my brother and sister and mother."

title: Matthew 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 That same day, Jesus went out of the house and sat by the sea.
2 Such large crowds gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat down. All the people stood on the shore.
3 He told them many things in parables, saying, "A farmer went out to plant his seed.
4 As he was planting, some seed fell along the path. The birds came and ate it up.
5 Some seed fell on rocky ground, where there was little soil. It grew up quickly because the soil was shallow.
6 But when the sun came up, the young plants were burned. They dried up because they had no roots.
7 Other seed fell among thorns. The thorns grew up and choked the young plants.
8 Still other seed fell on good soil. It produced a crop — 100, 60, or 30 times more.
9 Whoever has ears, let him hear."
10 Then the disciples came to Jesus and asked, "Why do you speak to the people in parables?"
11 He replied, "The secrets of the kingdom of heaven have been given to you, but not to them.
12 Whoever has will be given more, and he will have more than enough. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.
13 This is why I speak to them in parables: 'Though seeing, they do not see. Though hearing, they do not hear or understand.'
14 In them, the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled: 'You will always be hearing but never understanding. You will always be seeing but never knowing.

15 For this people's heart has grown hard. They hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise, they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, and turn, and I would heal them.'
16 But blessed are your eyes, because they see. And blessed are your ears, because they hear.
17 I tell you the truth: many prophets and righteous men wished for a long time to see what you see, but did not see it. They wished to hear what you hear, but did not hear it.
18 So listen to the parable of the farmer:
19 When anyone hears the message of the kingdom but does not understand it, the evil one comes and takes away what was planted in his heart. This is the seed planted along the path.
20 The seed planted on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.
21 But since he has no root, he lasts only for a short time. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, he quickly falls away.
22 The seed planted among the thorns is the one who hears the word. But the worries of this life and the false promises of money choke the word, and it does not produce fruit.
23 But the seed planted on good soil is the one who hears the word and understands it. He produces fruit and gives a crop — 100, 60, or 30 times more."
24 Jesus told them another parable: "The kingdom of heaven is like a man who planted good seed in his field.
25 But while everyone was sleeping, his enemy came and planted weeds among the wheat, and went away.
26 When the wheat grew up and produced grain, the weeds also appeared.
27 The owner's servants came to him and said, 'Sir, didn't you plant good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?'
28 'An enemy did this,' he replied. So the servants asked him, 'Do you want us to go and pull them up?'
29 'No,' he said. 'If you pull the weeds now, you might pull up the wheat with them.
30 Let both grow together until the harvest. At that time, I will tell the harvesters: First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned. Then gather the wheat into my barn.'"
31 He told them another parable: "The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man planted in his field.
32 It is the smallest of all seeds. Yet it grows into the largest of garden plants and becomes a tree. The birds of the sky come and rest in its branches."
33 He told them still another parable: "The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour, until all of it was full of yeast."
34 Jesus spoke all these things to the crowds in parables. He did not tell them anything without using a parable.
35 So was fulfilled what was spoken through the prophet: "I will open my mouth in parables. I will speak things hidden since the world began."
36 Then Jesus sent the crowds away and went into the house. His disciples came to him and said, "Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field."
37 He replied, "The One who plants the good seed is the Son of Man.
38 The field is the world. The good seed represents the sons of the kingdom. The weeds are the sons of the evil one.
39 The enemy who plants them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the harvesters are angels.
40 As the weeds are collected and burned in the fire, so will it be at the end of the age.
41 The Son of Man will send out his angels. They will take out of his kingdom everything that causes sin and all those who break the law.
42 They will throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.
43 Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. Whoever has ears, let him hear.
44 The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it, he hid it again. Then in his joy, he went and sold all he had and bought that field.
45 Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant searching for fine pearls.
46 When he found one very precious pearl, he went away and sold all he had and bought it.
47 Once again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that was thrown into the sea. It caught all kinds of fish.
48 When it was full, the men pulled it ashore. They sat down and sorted the good fish into containers, but threw the bad away.
49 So it will be at the end of the age. The angels will come and separate the wicked from the righteous.
50 They will throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.
51 Have you understood all these things?" "Yes," they answered.
52 Then he told them, "So every scribe who has been trained in the kingdom of heaven is like a homeowner who brings out of his storeroom new treasures and old."
53 When Jesus had finished these parables, he went away from there.
54 Coming to his hometown, he taught the people in their synagogue. They were amazed. "Where did this man get such wisdom and miraculous powers?" they asked.
55 "Isn't this the carpenter's son? Isn't his mother's name Mary? Aren't his brothers James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas?
56 Aren't all his sisters here with us? Where then did this man get all these things?"
57 And they refused to accept him. But Jesus said to them, "A prophet has honor everywhere — except in his hometown and in his own household."
58 And he did not do many miracles there, because of their lack of faith.

title: Matthew 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 At that time, Herod the tetrarch heard the reports about Jesus.
2 He said to his servants, "This is John the Baptist. He has risen from the dead! That is why miraculous powers are working in him."
3 Herod had arrested John, tied him up, and put him in prison. He did this because of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife.
4 John had been telling him, "It is not right for you to have her."
5 Although Herod wanted to kill John, he was afraid of the people, because they thought of John as a prophet.
6 But on Herod's birthday, the daughter of Herodias danced before them. She pleased Herod
7 so much that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she asked.
8 Told by her mother, she said, "Give me here on a plate the head of John the Baptist."
9 The king was very sad. But because of his promises and his guests, he ordered that her wish be granted.
10 He sent to have John's head cut off in the prison.
11 John's head was brought in on a plate and given to the girl. She carried it to her mother.
12 Then John's disciples came and took his body and buried it. They went and told Jesus.
13 When Jesus heard about John, he went away in a boat to a quiet place by himself. But the crowds found out about it and followed him on foot from the towns.
14 When he stepped ashore and saw a large crowd, he felt sorry for them and healed their sick.
15 When evening came, the disciples came to him and said, "This is an empty place, and it is already late. Send the crowds away so they can go to the villages and buy themselves some food."
16 "They do not need to go away," Jesus replied. "You give them something to eat."
17 "We have here only five loaves of bread and two fish," they answered.
18 "Bring them here to me," Jesus said.

19 He told the crowds to sit down on the grass. Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, he said a blessing. Then he broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples. The disciples gave them to the people.
20 They all ate and were full. The disciples picked up twelve baskets of leftover pieces.
21 About 5,000 men were fed, besides women and children.
22 Right away, Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go on ahead of him to the other side. He stayed to send the crowds away.
23 After he had sent them away, he went up on the mountain by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone.
24 But the boat was already far from land, beaten by the waves, because the wind was against it.
25 Very early in the morning, Jesus went out to them, walking on the sea.
26 When the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were terrified. "It's a ghost!" they said, and cried out in fear.
27 But Jesus spoke to them right away. "Be brave!" he said. "It is me. Do not be afraid."
28 "Lord, if it is you," Peter replied, "tell me to come to you on the water."
29 "Come," said Jesus. Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water, and came toward Jesus.
30 But when he saw the strong wind, he was afraid. He began to sink and cried out, "Lord, save me!"
31 Right away, Jesus reached out his hand and took hold of Peter. "You of little faith," he said, "why did you doubt?"
32 When they had climbed back into the boat, the wind died down.
33 Then those who were in the boat worshiped him. They said, "Truly you are the Son of God!"
34 When they had crossed over, they landed at Gennesaret.
35 When the men of that place recognized Jesus, they sent word to all the surrounding area. People brought all the sick to him.
36 They begged him to let them just touch the edge of his coat. And all who touched him were healed.

title: Matthew 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 Then some Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and asked,
2 "Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They do not wash their hands before they eat."
3 Jesus replied, "And why do you break the command of God for the sake of your tradition?
4 For God said, 'Honor your father and mother,' and, 'Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.'
5 But you say that if anyone says to his father or mother, 'Whatever you would have received from me is a gift devoted to God,'
6 he need not honor his father or mother with it. So you cancel the word of God for the sake of your tradition.
7 You hypocrites! Isaiah spoke truly about you:
8 'These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me.
9 They worship me for nothing. They teach the rules of men as if they were God's teachings.'"
10 Jesus called the crowd to him and said, "Listen and understand.
11 A person is not made unclean by what enters his mouth. He is made unclean by what comes out of it."
12 Then the disciples came to him and said, "Do you know that the Pharisees were upset when they heard this?"
13 But Jesus replied, "Every plant that my heavenly Father has not planted will be pulled up by its roots.
14 Leave them alone! They are blind guides. If a blind man leads a blind man, both will fall into a pit."
15 Peter said to him, "Explain this parable to us."
16 "Do you still not understand?" Jesus asked.
17 "Don't you see that whatever enters the mouth goes into the stomach and then passes out of the body?
18 But the things that come out of the mouth come from the heart. These things make a person unclean.
19 For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, sexual sins, stealing, false testimony, and saying bad things about people.
20 These are what make a person unclean. But eating with unwashed hands does not make him unclean."
21 Leaving that place, Jesus went to the area of Tyre and Sidon.
22 A Canaanite woman from that region came to him, crying out, "Lord, Son of David, have mercy on me! My daughter is terribly controlled by a demon."
23 But Jesus did not answer a word. So his disciples came and begged him, "Send her away, for she keeps crying out after us."
24 He answered, "I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel."
25 The woman came and knelt before him. "Lord, help me!" she said.
26 But Jesus replied, "It is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs."
27 "Yes, Lord," she said, "even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their master's table."
28 "O woman," Jesus answered, "your faith is great! Let it be done for you as you wish." And her daughter was healed from that very hour.
29 Moving on from there, Jesus went along the Sea of Galilee. Then he went up on a mountain and sat down.
30 Large crowds came to him. They brought the lame, the blind, the crippled, those who could not speak, and many others. They laid them at his feet, and he healed them.
31 The crowd was amazed when they saw those who could not speak now speaking, the crippled made well, the lame walking, and the blind seeing. They gave glory to the God of Israel.

32 Then Jesus called his disciples to him and said, "I feel sorry for this crowd. They have already been with me three days and have nothing to eat. I do not want to send them away hungry, or they may become very weak along the way."
33 The disciples replied, "Where in this empty place could we find enough bread to feed such a large crowd?"
34 "How many loaves do you have?" Jesus asked. "Seven," they replied, "and a few small fish."
35 He told the crowd to sit down on the ground.
36 Taking the seven loaves and the fish, he gave thanks and broke them. He gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people.
37 They all ate and were full. The disciples picked up seven baskets of leftover pieces.
38 A total of 4,000 men were fed, besides women and children.
39 After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into the boat and went to the region of Magadan.

title: Matthew 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 Then the Pharisees and Sadducees came and tested Jesus. They asked him to show them a sign from heaven.
2 But he replied, "When evening comes, you say, 'The weather will be fair, for the sky is red.'
3 And in the morning, 'Today it will be stormy, for the sky is red and cloudy.' You know how to read the appearance of the sky, but you cannot read the signs of the times.
4 An evil and unfaithful generation demands a sign. But none will be given it except the sign of Jonah." Then he left them and went away.
5 When they crossed to the other side, the disciples forgot to take bread.
6 "Watch out!" Jesus told them. "Be careful of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees."
7 They talked about this among themselves and decided, "It is because we did not bring any bread."
8 Jesus knew what they were saying. He said, "You of little faith, why are you talking among yourselves about having no bread?
9 Do you still not understand? Don't you remember the five loaves for the 5,000, and how many baskets you gathered?
10 Or the seven loaves for the 4,000, and how many baskets you gathered?
11 How do you not understand that I was not telling you about bread? Be careful of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees."
12 Then they understood that he was not telling them to be careful of the yeast used in bread. He was telling them to be careful of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
13 When Jesus came to the area of Caesarea Philippi, he questioned his disciples: "Who do people say the Son of Man is?"
14 They replied, "Some say John the Baptist. Others say Elijah. Still others say Jeremiah or one of the prophets."
15 "But what about you?" Jesus asked. "Who do you say I am?"
16 Simon Peter answered, "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God."
17 Jesus replied, "Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah! For this was not shown to you by any person, but by my Father in heaven.
18 And I tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church. The gates of Hades will not win against it.
19 I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven. Whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven."
20 Then he warned the disciples not to tell anyone that he was the Christ.

21 From that time on, Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem. He must suffer many things at the hands of the elders, chief priests, and scribes. He must be killed, and on the third day be raised to life.
22 Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him. "This must never happen to you, Lord!" he said. "This will never happen to you!"
23 But Jesus turned and said to Peter, "Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me. You are not thinking the things of God, but the things of men."
24 Then Jesus told his disciples, "If anyone wants to follow me, he must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.
25 Whoever wants to save his life will lose it. But whoever loses his life for my sake will find it.
26 What will it benefit a person to gain the whole world, but lose his soul? Or what can a person give in exchange for his soul?
27 For the Son of Man will come in his Father's glory with his angels. Then he will repay each person for what they have done.
28 I tell you the truth: some who are standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming in his kingdom."

title: Matthew 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 Six days later, Jesus took Peter, James, and John (the brother of James) with him. He led them up a high mountain by themselves.
2 There his appearance was changed before them. His face shone like the sun. His clothes became as white as light.
3 Suddenly, Moses and Elijah appeared before them. They were talking with Jesus.
4 Peter said to Jesus, "Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you wish, I will put up three shelters — one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah."
5 While Peter was still speaking, a bright cloud covered them. A voice from the cloud said, "This is my beloved Son, in whom I am very pleased. Listen to him!"
6 When the disciples heard this, they fell facedown in fear.
7 Then Jesus came over and touched them. "Get up," he said. "Do not be afraid."
8 When they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
9 As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, "Do not tell anyone about this vision until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead."
10 The disciples asked him, "Why then do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"
11 Jesus replied, "Elijah does indeed come, and he will bring all things back to order.
12 But I tell you that Elijah has already come. They did not recognize him, but did to him whatever they wished. In the same way, the Son of Man will suffer at their hands."
13 Then the disciples understood that he was speaking to them about John the Baptist.
14 When they came to the crowd, a man came up to Jesus and knelt before him.
15 "Lord, have mercy on my son," he said. "He has seizures and is suffering terribly. He often falls into the fire or into the water.
16 I brought him to your disciples, but they could not heal him."
17 "You unbelieving and twisted generation!" Jesus replied. "How long must I stay with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy here to me."
18 Then Jesus ordered the demon, and it came out of the boy. The boy was healed from that moment.
19 Afterward, the disciples came to Jesus in private and asked, "Why couldn't we drive it out?"

20 "Because you have so little faith," he answered. "I tell you the truth: if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you."
21 [Some manuscripts add: "But this kind does not go out except by prayer and fasting."]
22 When they gathered together in Galilee, Jesus told them, "The Son of Man is about to be handed over to men.
23 They will kill him, and on the third day he will be raised to life." And the disciples were very sad.
24 When they had arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the two-drachma tax came to Peter. They asked, "Does your Teacher pay the two drachmas?"

25 "Yes," he answered. When Peter entered the house, Jesus spoke first. "What do you think, Simon?" he asked. "From whom do the kings of the earth collect customs and taxes — from their own sons, or from others?"
26 "From others," Peter answered. "Then the sons do not have to pay," Jesus said.

27 "But so that we don't upset them, go to the sea. Throw out a fishing line and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a four-drachma coin. Take it and give it to them for my tax and yours."

title: Matthew 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 At that time, the disciples came to Jesus and asked, "Who then is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?"
2 Jesus called a little child to stand among them.
3 "I tell you the truth," he said, "unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
4 So whoever makes himself humble like this little child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
5 And whoever welcomes a little child like this in my name welcomes me.
6 But if anyone makes one of these little ones who believe in me fall into sin, it would be better for him to have a large millstone hung around his neck and to be drowned in the deep sea.
7 How terrible for the world because of the things that cause sin! These things must come, but how terrible for the person through whom they come!

8 If your hand or your foot makes you sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better to enter life crippled or lame than to have two hands and two feet and be thrown into the fire that lasts forever.
9 And if your eye makes you sin, tear it out and throw it away. It is better to enter life with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fire of hell.
10 See that you do not despise any of these little ones. I tell you that their angels in heaven always see the face of my Father in heaven.
11 [Some manuscripts add: "For the Son of Man came to save what was lost."]
12 What do you think? If a man has a hundred sheep, and one of them wanders away, will he not leave the ninety-nine on the hills and go out to search for the one that is lost?
13 And if he finds it, I tell you the truth: he rejoices more over that one sheep than over the ninety-nine that did not wander away.
14 In the same way, your Father in heaven is not willing that any of these little ones should be lost.
15 If your brother sins against you, go and tell him about it in private. If he listens to you, you have brought your brother back.
16 But if he will not listen, take one or two others along, so that 'every matter may be confirmed by the words of two or three witnesses.'
17 If he refuses to listen to them, tell it to the church. And if he refuses to listen even to the church, regard him as you would a pagan or a tax collector.
18 I tell you the truth: whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven. Whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.
19 Again, I tell you the truth: if two of you on the earth agree about anything you ask for, it will be done for you by my Father in heaven.
20 For where two or three gather together in my name, there I am with them."
21 Then Peter came to Jesus and asked, "Lord, how many times shall I forgive my brother when he sins against me? Up to seven times?"
22 Jesus answered, "I tell you, not just seven times, but seventy-seven times!
23 Because of this, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to check the records with his servants.
24 As he began checking, a man who owed him 10,000 talents was brought to him.
25 Since the man could not pay, the master ordered that he be sold to pay his debt, along with his wife and children and everything he owned.
26 Then the servant fell on his knees before him. 'Be patient with me,' he begged, 'and I will pay back everything.'
27 His master felt sorry for him. He forgave his debt and let him go.
28 But when that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him 100 denarii. He grabbed him and began to choke him, saying, 'Pay back what you owe me!'
29 So his fellow servant fell down and begged him, 'Be patient with me, and I will pay you back.'
30 But he refused. Instead, he went and had the man thrown into prison until he could pay his debt.
31 When his fellow servants saw what had happened, they were very upset. They went and reported all of this to their master.
32 Then the master called him in. 'You evil servant!' he said. 'I forgave all your debt because you begged me.
33 Shouldn't you have shown mercy to your fellow servant, just as I showed mercy to you?'
34 In anger, his master turned him over to the jailers to be tortured, until he could repay all that he owed.
35 That is how my heavenly Father will treat each of you, unless you forgive your brother from your heart."

title: Matthew 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 When Jesus had finished saying these things, he left Galilee and went into the region of Judea, east of the Jordan.
2 Large crowds followed him, and he healed them there.
3 Then some Pharisees came and tested him by asking, "Is it allowed by the law for a man to divorce his wife for any reason?"
4 Jesus answered, "Have you not read that from the beginning the Creator 'made them male and female,'
5 and said, 'For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two will become one flesh'?
6 So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate."
7 "Why then," they asked, "did Moses order a man to give his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away?"
8 Jesus replied, "Moses allowed you to divorce your wives because of your hard hearts. But it was not this way from the beginning.
9 I tell you: whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual sin, and marries another woman, commits adultery."
10 His disciples said to him, "If this is the situation between a man and his wife, it is better not to marry."
11 "Not everyone can accept this word," he replied. "Only those to whom it has been given.

12 For there are eunuchs who were born that way. Others were made that way by men. And still others live like eunuchs for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. The one who can accept this should accept it."
13 Then the little children were brought to Jesus, so that he could place his hands on them and pray for them. But the disciples scolded those who brought them.
14 Jesus said, "Let the little children come to me. Do not stop them! For the kingdom of heaven belongs to people like these."
15 After he had placed his hands on them, he went on from there.
16 Just then a man came up to Jesus and asked, "Teacher, what good thing must I do to get eternal life?"
17 "Why do you ask me about what is good?" Jesus replied. "There is only One who is good. If you want to enter life, keep the commandments."
18 "Which ones?" the man asked. Jesus answered, "'Do not murder. Do not commit adultery. Do not steal. Do not give false testimony.
19 Honor your father and mother. And love your neighbor as yourself.'"
20 "I have kept all these," the young man said. "What do I still lack?"
21 Jesus told him, "If you want to be perfect, go and sell what you own and give to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me."
22 When the young man heard this, he went away in sorrow, because he was very rich.
23 Then Jesus said to his disciples, "I tell you the truth: it is hard for a rich man to enter the kingdom of heaven.
24 Again I tell you: it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God."
25 When the disciples heard this, they were very amazed and asked, "Then who can be saved?"
26 Jesus looked at them and said, "With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible."
27 "Look," Peter replied, "we have left everything to follow you. What then will there be for us?"

28 Jesus said to them, "I tell you the truth: when all things are made new and the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
29 Everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or wife or children or fields for the sake of my name will receive 100 times as much. And he will inherit eternal life.
30 But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.

title: Matthew 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 "For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire workers for his vineyard.
2 He agreed to pay them a denarius for the day, and sent them into his vineyard.
3 About the third hour, he went out and saw others standing in the marketplace doing nothing.
4 'You also go into my vineyard,' he said. 'I will pay you whatever is right.'
5 So they went. He went out again about the sixth hour and the ninth hour, and did the same thing.
6 About the eleventh hour, he went out and found still others standing around. 'Why have you been standing here all day doing nothing?' he asked.
7 'Because no one has hired us,' they answered. So he told them, 'You also go into my vineyard.'
8 When evening came, the owner of the vineyard said to his foreman, 'Call the workers and pay them their wages. Start with the last ones hired and move on to the first.'
9 The workers who were hired about the eleventh hour came, and each received a denarius.
10 So when the first workers came, they thought they would receive more. But each of them also received a denarius.
11 When they received their pay, they began to complain against the landowner.
12 'These men who were hired last worked only one hour,' they said, 'and you have made them equal to us. We carried the load and the burning heat of the day.'
13 But he answered one of them, 'Friend, I am not being unfair to you. Did you not agree with me on one denarius?
14 Take your pay and go. I want to give this last man the same as I gave you.
15 Don't I have the right to do what I want with what is mine? Or are you jealous because I am generous?'
16 So the last will be first, and the first will be last."
17 As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside and said,
18 "Look, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and scribes. They will say he deserves to die.
19 They will hand him over to the Gentiles to be made fun of, whipped, and crucified. And on the third day, he will be raised to life."
20 Then the mother of Zebedee's sons came to Jesus with her sons. She knelt down to ask him for something.
21 "What do you want?" he asked. She answered, "Promise that in your kingdom, one of these two sons of mine may sit at your right hand, and the other at your left."
22 "You do not know what you are asking," Jesus replied. "Can you drink the cup I am going to drink?" "We can," the brothers answered.
23 "You will indeed drink my cup," Jesus said. "But to sit at my right or left is not mine to give. These seats belong to those for whom my Father has prepared them."
24 When the ten heard about this, they were angry with the two brothers.
25 But Jesus called them aside and said, "You know that the rulers of the Gentiles act like rulers over them. Their leaders use their power on them.
26 It must not be this way among you. Whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant.
27 And whoever wants to be first among you must be your slave —
28 just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many."
29 As they were leaving Jericho, a large crowd followed him.
30 Two blind men were sitting beside the road. When they heard that Jesus was passing by, they cried out, "Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!"
31 The crowd told them to be quiet, but they cried out all the louder, "Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!"
32 Jesus stopped and called them. "What do you want me to do for you?" he asked.
33 "Lord," they answered, "let our eyes be opened."
34 Jesus felt deep pity. He touched their eyes, and at once they could see. They followed him.

title: Matthew 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Matthew
paragraphs: 1 As they came near Jerusalem and reached Bethphage on the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent out two disciples.
2 He said to them, "Go into the village ahead of you. At once you will find a donkey tied there, with her colt beside her. Untie them and bring them to me.
3 If anyone questions you, tell him that the Lord needs them. He will send them right away."
4 This took place to make true what was spoken through the prophet:
5 "Say to the Daughter of Zion, 'See, your King comes to you, gentle and riding on a donkey, on a colt, the young of a donkey.'"
6 So the disciples went and did as Jesus had told them.
7 They brought the donkey and the colt, and laid their coats on them. Jesus sat on them.
8 A huge crowd spread their coats on the road. Others cut branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
9 The crowds that went ahead of him and those that followed were shouting: "Hosanna to the Son of David!" "Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!" "Hosanna in the highest!"
10 When Jesus had entered Jerusalem, the whole city was excited and asked, "Who is this?"
11 The crowds replied, "This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee."
12 Then Jesus entered the temple area and drove out all who were buying and selling there. He turned over the tables of the money changers and the seats of those selling doves.
13 He said to them, "It is written: 'My house will be called a house of prayer.' But you are making it 'a hideout of robbers.'"
